evidence.text: Since returning to the UK, there have been no further events reported.
The patient describes seizure activity occurring in flurries over several days with intervals of relative calm thereafter, i.e. a stop–start pattern rather than a steady baseline.

He describes these as short spells of disorientation with a metallic taste and a need to pause for 30–60 seconds, without loss of awareness, sometimes repeating two or three times within the same morning.
The patient reports being free of events for the past seven months
She had no seizures for nearly a year following initiation of Valproate, then developed myoclonic jerks leading to a tonic seizure three Saturdays ago.
Electrographic seizures frequent on EEG (~4/h) were recorded during a recent ambulatory study
the family have maintained a detailed diary and confirm 4 clusters this quarter
She reports nocturnal episodes occurring once per night on average for the past three months
"Over the past two months she reports six drop attacks and two absence seizures in the past two months"

Regarding frequency, the patient reports that episodes tend to come in small runs: typically around four to six short spells grouped together on days when they occur, most commonly later in the day towards the evening period. There may then be several days without events.
No further tonic-clonic seizures have occurred since 12/2020, although two to three single jerks remain, typically noted at the end of long shifts or on waking after reduced sleep.
Patient reports 2 clusters this month; each ≈4 absences in the morning.
events occurring q1 - 2d
his last episode was recorded on 26 February and he has remained well since. Considering that he has now been free of seizures for three weeks
The patient now reports brief focal impaired-awareness seizures occurring every 2 days
electrographic seizures frequent on EEG (~6/h)
Over the last eight weeks, simple partial seizures happen after lack of sleep, typically one to two events in the morning following nights with less than five hours of rest.
he continues to suffer one to two generalised tonic-clonic seizures per month
reports five focal sensory and six focal non-motors in last month
In Nov he had 3 seizures during sleep and 1 while awake. In Jan he had five in sleep and one while awake.
She reports one absence seizure every one to two day
She had 7 convulsions so far in Sep, 4 in Aug, 2 in Jul, one in Jun both from being awake and asleep.

In November he had a run of one seizure within half an hour (not full generalised tonic-clonic, fluctuating in intensity, resolved without medication). In December there was 7 further seizures at night, and in March another during physiotherapy (unwitnessed by mother).

Over the past several months there has been some fluctuation, partially coinciding with disrupted sleep and delayed medication times during fasting periods. He had a cluster of three seizures in August (short, not full convulsions, fluctuating awareness, self-terminating). In October he had six nocturnal seizures, and in January a single tonic seizure was recorded during respite care.
just seven generalised tonic-clonic seizures documented this year to date
Patient reports two clusters this quarter... Within each cluster, events were brief focal aware seizures
with just five generalised tonic-clonic seizures documented this year to date
cluster burden increased since 1 January 2024; now weekly, two per cluster.
In Oct he had no nocturnal seizures but 5 daytime events. In December he had two nocturnal seizures and two while awake.
Over the past three weeks he has been experiencing seizures twice a day
she continues to have nightly generalised convulsions seizures
The patient reports brief episodes of loss of awareness occurring abs *monthly
episodes are now tending to occur at most on a monthly basis
with a current frequency of ≤ 3 per day on days with prolonged screen exposure and irregular sleep
She noted a seizure in Sep, 2 in Aug, and four in Jul
He reports "sz *three/d" on average during evening shifts
In Oct she had no nocturnal seizures but 2 daytime events. In Nov she had a nocturnal seizure and 5 while awake.
Current average frequency is 9 per month
he has experienced 2 to 3 drop attacks during the last two weeks
Morning clusters 2×/month; ~2 to 4 events over 90 min.

values.normalized_value: 2 to 4 cluster per month, 2 to 4 per cluster
values.raw_value: 2 to 4 cluster per month, 2 to 4 per cluster

evidence.text: Given that there have been no further episodes in the past month and his epilepsy is otherwise stable
having been seizure free for years
No convulsive events reported in the past year.
Seizure diary indicates clusters on high‑stress maintenance nights, with an Occasional prolonged seizure event up to 12 minutes witnessed by colleagues
she continues to have nightly generalised tonic-clonic seizures
Over the past four months, the patient reports that the Median inter-seizure interval ≈ three weeks
there have been effectively no spell-like events suggestive of seizures over the past six months
Generalised tonic-clonic seizures are rare, typically 3 events per year.
brief episodes occur daily
she has had two drop attacks and five tonic-clonic in the past three months
focal impaired-awareness seizures have been occurring more often, 2 times per week
Clusters characterized by two - three focal impaired-awareness seizures; frequency unclear.
Her last reported event was on 24 Jul and she has been seizure-free since.
Since our last contact he had two brief focal aware seizures associated with a red-eye flight to North America and a subsequent return journey
Over the past three months she has noticed a consistent pattern of Morning clusters 3×/month
During this timeframe she notes no auras, no brief staring spells, and no convulsive episodes.
No further seizures have occurred since.
Drop attacks are now reported six times weekly
Over the last month he reports two focal sensory and one focal non-motor in last month
there has been no further event since early February
He estimates the more disruptive events occur q2 - 3wk
His absence seizures are now occurring on three days of the week
the current pattern is focal sensory once every two days
they continue to have seizures every 4 weeks
Weekly morning clusters reported; number per cluster not documented.
He continues to suffer focal aware seizures few times per month
during flares he experiences multiple events, with a reported frequency of up to seven in bad weeks
Over the last two months he reports TC X9/wk
≤ 2 seizures per day
he does not keep a diary and finds it hard to quantify overall frequency. When clusters occur, typically two - three absences over ~1 h; frequency not tracked.
He explained that since discharge from hospital he has experienced several seizures, the last one being on 20 February.
He reports a myoclonic jerk every other week
The patient reports about three - ten seizures in the last two weeks

The patient reports greater functional impact with more frequent post-ictal fatigue and difficulty maintaining work attendance. According to the patient a seizure occurred on 09-Feb, followed by several brief events over the subsequent two weeks, predominantly in the perimenstrual window.
She has remained seizure-free since then.
frequency up to seven in bad weeks
The history suggests a possible cyclical influence on seizure occurrence, with several clusters aligning with the late luteal phase according to the app’s logged cycles.
he continues to suffer one to two generalised tonic-clonic seizures per month

She reports that her typical event begins with a brief rising epigastric sensation and déjà vu lasting 15–30 seconds, occasionally followed by impaired awareness with automatisms and a short post-event confusion.
over the past three weeks she has experienced about seven to nine seizures in the last three weeks
no seizure frequency reference
she continues to experience epileptic spasm on a daily basis

no unprovoked episodes for over 12 months
She reports a recent shift in her seizure pattern over the past three months, characterised by clusters arising after nights of curtailed sleep
He has remained stable without seizures since.
Frequency increased by ~20% after dose increase.
seizure freedom since 25 December 2023
Focal seizure: 2022: Aug x0, Sep x2, Oct x1, Nov x2, Dec x0. 2023: Jan x1, Feb x1, Mar x1, Apr x2.
He reports Monthly clusters, typically 3 - 10 seizures over 24 h

evidence.text: Frequency currently unclear — episodes tend to occur around times of inconsistent antiepileptic use (patient-reported), with no reliable count available.
He reports that since titration to levetiracetam 1000 mg twice daily in August 2023, there have been no further events suggestive of seizures.

He previously suffered from frequent clusters of myoclonic jerks, absence seizures and occasional convulsive events three times per year, but is currently in long-term remission, having been seizure free for years.
he has had 3 - 4 generalised tonic-clonic seizures, the most recent on 23 December
